Scenario.title: ShadowRay Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This scenario outlines a targeted attack campaign known as ShadowRay exploiting vulnerabilities in Internet-facing assets through spearphishing links and leveraging exploits in SCADA systems to manipulate operational controls and cause impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target System Artifact, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Manipulation of Control

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 467\nName: Cross Site Identification\nDescription: An attacker harvests identifying information about a victim via an active session that the victim's browser has with a social networking site. A victim may have the social networking site open in one tab or perhaps is simply using the remember functionality."
stage.description: An authenticated employee interacts with a malicious link embedded within a highly targeted spearphishing email designed specifically for their position or access privileges. This action allows the attacker to compromise the employee's authentication token or establish persistence on the compromised system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 67\nName: String Format Overflow in syslog()\nDescription: This attack targets applications and software that uses the syslog() function insecurely. If an application does not explicitly use a format string parameter in a call to syslog(), user input can be placed in the format string parameter, resulting in log message manipulation or arbitrary command execution."
stage.description: Leveraging a remote code execution vulnerability commonly found in older versions of Supervisory Control and Manufacturing Protocol (SCADA) systems or related industrial control system (ICS) components, often due to unpatched software or misconfigurations. This allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands or code on the target device.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 166\nName: Force the System to Reset Values\nDescription: An attacker forces the target into a previous state in order to leverage potential weaknesses in the target dependent upon a prior configuration or state-dependent factors. Even in cases where an attacker may not be able to directly change values, forcing a reset can alter operational parameters."
stage.description: Once remote code execution is achieved, attackers modify settings on critical equipment such as pumps, valves, or chemical dosing sensors. This could involve changing operational speeds, bypassing safety interlocks, or altering process parameters to disrupt normal functioning, potentially causing overflow, environmental hazards, or denial of service to water treatment facilities.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay campaign achieves simple persistence by compromising an endpoint via spearphishing, then escalating privileges implicitly through RCE in vulnerable SCADA systems, ultimately aiming to disrupt water/wastewater operations by manipulating control logic.
